mission.title: Install and Configure ClickHouse
mission.description: ClickHouse is a real-time analytics database management system designed for generating analytical data reports efficiently. It is ideal for applications requiring high-speed data processing and real-time analytics. Key trade-offs include the need for careful schema design and potential complexity in managing distributed setups.

steps.title: Add ClickHouse Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official ClickHouse Helm repository to your local Helm setup, allowing you to install ClickHouse using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add clickhouse https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the ClickHouse Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository to ensure you have the latest charts.

steps.title: Install ClickHouse with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs ClickHouse in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install clickhouse clickhouse/clickhouse --namespace clickhouse --create-namespace --version 26.2.4.23-stable

```

> Installs ClickHouse in the 'clickhouse' namespace.

steps.title: Create ClickHouse Configuration
steps.description:
This step creates a basic ClickHouse configuration to define the cluster settings.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: clickhouse.altinity.com/v1
kind: ClickHouseCluster
metadata:
  name: clickhouse
spec:
  configuration:
    clusters:
      - name: cluster
        shardsCount: 1
        replicasCount: 1
EOF

```

> Creates a simple ClickHouse cluster configuration.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: clickhouse.altinity.com/v1
kind: ClickHouseCluster
metadata:
  name: clickhouse
spec:
  configuration:
    clusters:
      - name: cluster
        shardsCount: 1
        replicasCount: 1
EOF

steps.title: Verify ClickHouse Installation
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the ClickHouse pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n clickhouse

```

> Lists all pods in the 'clickhouse' namespace to verify their status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall ClickHouse
uninstall.description:
This step removes ClickHouse and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss unless backups are taken.

```bash

helm uninstall clickhouse --namespace clickhouse

kubectl delete namespace clickhouse

```

> Uninstalls the ClickHouse release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade ClickHouse
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step will upgrade ClickHouse to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl cp clickhouse-backup:/path/to/backup /local/path

helm upgrade clickhouse clickhouse/clickhouse --namespace clickhouse --version 26.2.4.23-stable

kubectl get pods -n clickhouse

```

> Backup existing data before the upgrade.
> Upgrades ClickHouse to the specified version.
> Verify that the ClickHouse pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'pod not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The ClickHouse pod may not have started correctly due to resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check the resource limits and ensure sufficient resources are allocated to the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The ClickHouse service may not be reachable due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration and ensure the correct ports are exposed.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'insufficient memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The ClickHouse pod is being evicted due to memory constraints.

**Fix:**
Increase the memory limits for the ClickHouse deployment.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced new query optimization features and improved data compression algorithms.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain legacy configuration options related to replication.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v25.x, review the new configuration options and adjust your settings accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see the ClickHouse pods running with the status 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n clickhouse'.

metadata:

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster set up with sufficient resources.
